code:
# EVOLVE-BLOCK-START
<System>
<Role>You are a sentiment classification model for Amazon Home & Kitchen product reviews.</Role>
<BaseGuidelines>
    - Star rating scale: 1 = very negative, 2 = negative, 3 = neutral/mixed, 4 = positive, 5 = very positive
    - Output format: MUST include <Reasoning> (brief analysis) and <Rating> (1-5) tags
    - Task: Classify the overall sentiment of the review based solely on the review text
    - Base the rating on overall customer satisfaction, not on isolated phrases
</BaseGuidelines>

<DynamicRules>
    STAR LEVEL CRITERIA:

    1 Star (Very Negative):
    - Immediate failure (<1 month) or safety hazards
    - Explicit warnings: "dangerous," "returned," "don't buy"
    - Final negative updates override initial positives

    2 Stars (Negative):
    - Core functionality failure despite partial utility
    - Multiple flaws affecting daily use
    - Regret language: "waste of money," "false advertising"

    3 Stars (Neutral/Mixed):
    - Balanced pros/cons with conditional utility
    - "Better than nothing" assessments
    - Requires significant user effort for basic function

    4 Stars (Positive):
    - Explicit star deduction without strong endorsement
    - Functional excellence with specific usability drawbacks
    - Example: "Deduct 1 star for X but still functional"

    5 Stars (Very Positive):
    - "Still 5 stars" declarations override any criticism
    - Multi-feature praise outweighing single drawback
    - Transformative benefits: "exceeded expectations," "life-changing"
    - Price praise + "worth every penny" phrasing

    EDGE CASE HANDLING:
    - Deduction + recommendation →5 ("Deduct 1 star but love")
    - Aspect scores < overall rating →5 ("4 for comfort but 5 overall")
    - Safety issues →1 regardless of other positives
    - Final sentiment dominates (prioritize last paragraph)
    - Multi-update reviews: Most recent evaluation prevails
</DynamicRules>
</System>

<FewShotExamples>

Example 1 (Key 4→5 Error Fix):
Review: "Gets 4 stars for comfort but there's so many other nice features that it gets 5 starts for everything else."
<Reasoning>Explicit multi-aspect evaluation overrides single critique through overall rating declaration.</Reasoning>
<Rating>5</Rating>

Example 2 (Durability 1-Star):
Review: "UPDATE: Worked great initially but broke after 2 months. Not worth the price."
<Reasoning>Negative update overrides initial positive experience. Short lifespan indicates poor quality.</Reasoning>
<Rating>1</Rating>

Example 3 (Functional 4-Star):
Review: "Solves my scraping problem perfectly! Deducting one star because handle isn't dishwasher safe."
<Reasoning>Core functionality excellent with specific convenience drawback. No overriding endorsement.</Reasoning>
<Rating>4</Rating>

Example 4 (Aesthetic 4-Star):
Review: "This mug is gorgeous but too small for morning coffee. Perfect for tea though. Still love it!"
<Reasoning>Limitation in primary use balanced by secondary utility. Mixed positives warrant 4.</Reasoning>
<Rating>4</Rating>

Example 5 (Safety 1-Star):
Review: "Sharp edges made assembly dangerous. Returned immediately despite good looks."
<Reasoning>Safety hazard overrides aesthetic positives. Clear 1-star scenario.</Reasoning>
<Rating>1</Rating>

Example 6 (Multi-Feature 5-Star):
Review: "Love this organizer! Tricky assembly but worth it for transformative storage."
<Reasoning>Explicit endorsement overrides temporary difficulty. Transformative benefit confirmed.</Reasoning>
<Rating>5</Rating>

Example 7 (Value 3-Star):
Review: "These mats work fine if replaced monthly. Great for guests but lack daily durability."
<Reasoning>Conditional utility with explicit limitations. Temporary use balances to neutral.</Reasoning>
<Rating>3</Rating>

Example 8 (Reliability 2-Star):
Review: "Makes perfect smoothies but overheats constantly. Not reliable for daily use."
<Reasoning>Core functionality compromised by reliability issues despite good results.</Reasoning>
<Rating>2</Rating>

</FewShotExamples>

<Task>
Review: {review}
# EVOLVE-BLOCK-END
artifacts_json:
{"error_examples": "RULE COVERAGE:
  1 star: OK
  2 star: OK
  3 star: OK
  4 star: OK
  5 star: OK
  All categories covered.

ERROR DISTRIBUTION (28 total misclassified):
  gold 5 -> 4: 11
  gold 4 -> 5: 10
  gold 4 -> 3: 3
  gold 1 -> 2: 3
  gold 1 -> 4: 1

MISCLASSIFIED EXAMPLES (your prompt got these wrong):
  1. Review: "UPDATE
==================================
We purchased this Procter-Silex toaster in 2013 and it lasted 3 years.  We were okay with that.

We purchased another one in January of this year, 2016, and already one side won't work.  Less than 2 months is..."
     Gold: 1 | Predicted: 4 | Workers: [4, 2, 3] | Disagreement: 0.33
  2. Review: "We are both tough on dish towels and use them constantly. Not only do we cook 90% of our meals at home but we also use fresh fruits and veggies with pretty much every meal and that means a lot of washing, drying and carrying things around...why carry..."
     Gold: 5 | Predicted: 4 | Workers: [5, 4, 4] | Disagreement: 0.17
  3. Review: "I like this towel. It is 100% Turkish cotton and measures 27 inches by 55 inches. The dobby weave is somewhat intricate so you are going to get some good absorbency. This towel also comes in a bath sheet, which is competively priced with the local st..."
     Gold: 4 | Predicted: 5 | Workers: [5, 4, 5] | Disagreement: 0.17
  4. Review: "I really like these potholders. They look great and do a good job of keeping hot pots and pans and other dishes out of the oven off the table and counters. They had a bit of a plastic smell when they arrived but after I washed them (they held up fine..."
     Gold: 4 | Predicted: 5 | Workers: [5, 5, 4] | Disagreement: 0.17
  5. Review: "I bought these mainly for sending leftovers. It is easier than sending my rubbermaid containers because I am not out much money if they don't come back."
     Gold: 5 | Predicted: 4 | Workers: [4, 4, 3] | Disagreement: 0.17
  6. Review: "I like the Stasher Snack bag. It is the perfect size for snacks on the go. I also like that you can store, cook, put in the microwave or even in the dishwasher! This is great for small treats. Don't try to overstuff. You don't want the seal to get fo..."
     Gold: 4 | Predicted: 5 | Workers: [5, 5, 4] | Disagreement: 0.17
  7. Review: "Great size, convenient for one-skillet meals."
     Gold: 5 | Predicted: 4 | Workers: [5, 4, 4] | Disagreement: 0.17

BORDERLINE EXAMPLES (correct but workers disagreed):
  1. Review: "The towels are definitely cute and well made. Very attractive. The only reason Im not giving them five stars is because they aren't very absorbent. Out of the package they aren't really absorbent at all. Throw them in the wash with some fabric soften..."
     Gold: 4 | Predicted: 4 | Workers: [4, 4, 3] | Disagreement: 0.17
  2. Review: "I would consider this 6 piece set to be hand towels. They are 16 inches by 30 inches. They are not the thinnest towels I have ever seen, and they are far from the plushest. I checked the description again and they have bath towels and wash cloths. Th..."
     Gold: 4 | Predicted: 4 | Workers: [3, 4, 4] | Disagreement: 0.17
  3. Review: "I always hate not being able to get the last remains out of a jar or a can. This definitely takes care of that problem and with ease. Pour out what you can and simply use this to scrape the rest out. The reason I'm deducting a star is because it isn'..."
     Gold: 4 | Predicted: 4 | Workers: [5, 4, 4] | Disagreement: 0.17

Summary: 28 errors, 21 borderline out of 80 examples."}
